title: vendor c/psgo@v1.7.1
body:
psgo added support for listing supplementary groups via
two new descriptors:

* `groups` for supplementary groups inside the container
* `hgroups` for the counterpart on the host

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

@rhatdan @giuseppe PTAL

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> @giuseppe, can you give guidance on the following error from `int podman fedora-33 root container`?

the cgroup seems strange:
```
[+0607s] Error: open /sys/fs/cgroup/pids//machine.slice/libpod-ec327f89e8ce48877bfd1316b867b047efa7bce87eafbbcf8ee5a882e922f8aa.scope/libpod_parent/libpod-029c7c0e8aa8fa941368dcb6e45b3160c093d3b538a6afa4e1a57bf927f2f5da/cgroup.procs: no such file or directory
```

When I run manually the same commands on a cgroup v1 machine I get a cgroup like:

```
12:hugetlb:/machine.slice/libpod-e8166fb1c1d9e0ba7946982feb3d6f3bdfaf1fef5c9038d3947ac6794c9004a6.scope
11:rdma:/
10:pids:/machine.slice/libpod-e8166fb1c1d9e0ba7946982feb3d6f3bdfaf1fef5c9038d3947ac6794c9004a6.scope
9:freezer:/machine.slice/libpod-e8166fb1c1d9e0ba7946982feb3d6f3bdfaf1fef5c9038d3947ac6794c9004a6.scope
8:devices:/machine.slice/libpod-e8166fb1c1d9e0ba7946982feb3d6f3bdfaf1fef5c9038d3947ac6794c9004a6.scope
7:cpuset:/machine.slice/libpod-e8166fb1c1d9e0ba7946982feb3d6f3bdfaf1fef5c9038d3947ac6794c9004a6.scope
6:perf_event:/machine.slice/libpod-e8166fb1c1d9e0ba7946982feb3d6f3bdfaf1fef5c9038d3947ac6794c9004a6.scope
5:memory:/machine.slice/libpod-e8166fb1c1d9e0ba7946982feb3d6f3bdfaf1fef5c9038d3947ac6794c9004a6.scope
4:net_cls,net_prio:/machine.slice/libpod-e8166fb1c1d9e0ba7946982feb3d6f3bdfaf1fef5c9038d3947ac6794c9004a6.scope
3:blkio:/machine.slice/libpod-e8166fb1c1d9e0ba7946982feb3d6f3bdfaf1fef5c9038d3947ac6794c9004a6.scope
2:cpu,cpuacct:/machine.slice/libpod-e8166fb1c1d9e0ba7946982feb3d6f3bdfaf1fef5c9038d3947ac6794c9004a6.scope
1:name=systemd:/machine.slice/libpod-e8166fb1c1d9e0ba7946982feb3d6f3bdfaf1fef5c9038d3947ac6794c9004a6.scope
```

Race condition with some other tests (not sure how it can happen though..)?

Could we use `session.OutputToString()` from the `container run` command to get the cid instead of using its name?

It always happens on `fedora-33 root container`.  The tests look good to me, a name conflict cannot happen since the containers get nuked in between.

I may just skip it in the containerized tests.

> It always happens on `fedora-33 root container`. The tests look good to me, a name conflict cannot happen since the containers get nuked in between.

ah right that is on containerized cgroup v1 and now it makes sense.  It is happening because on cgroup v1 runc breaks what `/proc/PID/cgroup` shows and where to read the cgroup (crun has the same issue to be compatible :cry: )

We can add a fallback when the path doesn't exist, to read from the root cgroup: https://github.com/containers/psgo/pull/90

I'll open a new PR to test it with Podman

> I'll open a new PR to test it with Podman

here: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/11679
Now vendoring v1.7.1 with @giuseppe's changes, thank you
